title: The Birth of Rama
summary: Rama is born in Ayodhya to King Dasharatha and Queen Kausalya after the Putrakameshti yajna.
kidSummary: After a sacred prayer, four princes are born in Ayodhya. Rama's birth fills the kingdom with joy.

seo.title: Rama Birth Story: Source, Meaning, and Rama Navami Activities
seo.description: Read the story of Rama's birth from the Ramayana tradition with source notes, kid-friendly meaning, quiz links, and Rama Navami activities.

aeo.shortAnswer: Rama was born in Ayodhya to King Dasharatha and Queen Kausalya after the Putrakameshti yajna.

title: Vishwamitra's Request
summary: The sage Vishwamitra asks Dasharatha to send Rama to protect his yajna, setting Rama on the path from palace childhood to public duty.
kidSummary: A great sage asks for Rama's help. Rama leaves the palace to learn courage and protect what is sacred.

seo.title: Vishwamitra's Request to Dasharatha: Rama's First Journey
seo.description: Learn why Vishwamitra asked for Rama and what this moment teaches about discipline, duty, and preparation.

aeo.shortAnswer: Vishwamitra asked Dasharatha to send Rama to protect his yajna from disruption.

title: Tataka Vadha
summary: Rama confronts Tataka under Vishwamitra's guidance, marking one of his first tests as a protector of dharma.
kidSummary: Rama learns that courage means protecting others even when the task feels difficult.

seo.title: Tataka Vadha: Rama's First Test in the Ramayana
seo.description: Understand the story of Tataka Vadha and why it matters in Rama's journey from prince to protector.

aeo.shortAnswer: Tataka Vadha is the episode where Rama confronts Tataka while accompanying Vishwamitra.

title: Ahalya's Liberation
summary: Rama's arrival brings release to Ahalya, a story remembered through themes of grace, restoration, and spiritual awakening.
kidSummary: Rama's presence helps Ahalya return to light. The story teaches that grace can restore what was forgotten.

seo.title: Ahalya's Liberation: Ramayana Story and Meaning
seo.description: Read the story of Ahalya's liberation with source notes, meaning, and kid-friendly explanation.

aeo.shortAnswer: Ahalya's liberation is a Ramayana episode where Rama's arrival restores Ahalya after a long period of penance.

title: Sita Swayamvar
summary: Rama lifts and strings Shiva's bow in Mithila, winning Sita's hand in the swayamvar.
kidSummary: In Mithila, Rama shows strength with humility and marries Sita.

seo.title: Sita Swayamvar: Rama, Sita, and Shiva's Bow
seo.description: Explore the Sita Swayamvar story from the Ramayana with meaning, characters, and kid-friendly activities.

aeo.shortAnswer: Sita Swayamvar is the episode where Rama wins Sita's hand by lifting and stringing Shiva's bow.

title: Rama's Exile
summary: Rama accepts fourteen years of exile to honor Dasharatha's promise, turning loss into an act of dharma.
kidSummary: Rama leaves the palace because keeping a promise matters more to him than becoming king.

seo.title: Rama's Exile: Why Rama Left Ayodhya
seo.description: Understand why Rama accepted exile, what it teaches about dharma, and how the episode shapes the Ramayana.

aeo.shortAnswer: Rama accepted exile to honor the promises connected to Dasharatha and Kaikeyi's boons.

title: Bharata's Vow
summary: Bharata refuses to take Rama's throne and rules Ayodhya only as Rama's representative.
kidSummary: Bharata shows love by keeping Rama's place safe until he returns.

seo.title: Bharata's Vow: Brotherhood and Dharma in the Ramayana
seo.description: Read the story of Bharata's vow and why it is one of the great examples of loyalty in the Ramayana.

aeo.shortAnswer: Bharata vowed not to take Rama's throne and ruled only as Rama's representative.

title: Hanuman Meets Rama
summary: Hanuman meets Rama and Lakshmana, beginning one of the most beloved relationships of devotion in Hindu tradition.
kidSummary: Hanuman meets Rama and becomes his brave, wise, and devoted helper.

seo.title: Hanuman Meets Rama: The Beginning of Devotion
seo.description: Learn how Hanuman meets Rama in the Ramayana and why the moment is central to devotion and service.

aeo.shortAnswer: Hanuman meets Rama in Kishkindha Kanda, beginning his service to Rama.

title: Dasharatha Meets Shani
summary: Dasharatha confronts Shani to protect the world from drought, and Shani grants a boon in response to the king's courage.
kidSummary: A brave king asks Shani Dev to protect ordinary people from suffering.

seo.title: Dasharatha and Shani Dev: Story, Meaning, and Karma Lesson
seo.description: Read the story of King Dasharatha meeting Shani Dev, with meaning, source notes, and a beginner quiz.

aeo.shortAnswer: In Puranic Shani traditions, Dasharatha confronts Shani to protect the world from drought and receives a protective boon.

title: The Birth of Shani
summary: Shani is remembered as the son of Surya and Chhaya, born from light and shadow and associated with karma, time, and discipline.
kidSummary: Shani Dev teaches that every action matters and that patience can make us stronger.

seo.title: Birth of Shani Dev: Surya, Chhaya, and the Meaning of Karma
seo.description: Learn the story of Shani Dev's birth, his connection to Surya and Chhaya, and what he teaches about karma.

aeo.shortAnswer: Shani Dev is remembered in Puranic traditions as the son of Surya and Chhaya, associated with karma, time, and discipline.

title: Why Ganesha Has an Elephant Head

sourceRefs.text: Shiva Purana and Puranic Ganesha traditions

summary: Parvati creates Ganesha to guard her privacy. Shiva, not recognizing him, strikes him down. The loss becomes repair when Ganesha is restored with an elephant head and honored before all beginnings.
kidSummary: Ganesha protects his mother's doorway. A mistake becomes a new beginning when he is restored and blessed.

seo.title: Why Ganesha Has an Elephant Head: Story, Meaning, and Activities
seo.description: Read the story of Ganesha's elephant head with meaning, parent discussion prompts, quiz, drawing kit, and Ganesh Chaturthi activities.

aeo.shortAnswer: Ganesha receives an elephant head after Shiva restores him and grants him honor before all beginnings.

title: Ganesha and the Fruit of Wisdom
summary: When Ganesha and Subrahmanya are challenged to circle the world, Ganesha circles his parents, seeing Shiva and Parvati as the living universe.
kidSummary: Ganesha wins through wisdom, not speed. He understands that love can reveal the whole world.

seo.title: Ganesha and the Fruit of Wisdom: Story and Meaning
seo.description: Explore the story where Ganesha wins through wisdom by circling Shiva and Parvati.

aeo.shortAnswer: Ganesha wins the fruit of wisdom by circling his parents, recognizing them as the world.

title: Matsya Avatar
summary: Vishnu appears as Matsya, the fish, to protect life, wisdom, and continuity through a cosmic flood.
kidSummary: Matsya teaches that wisdom must be protected when the world changes.

seo.title: Matsya Avatar Story: Meaning and Dashavatara Activities
seo.description: Learn the story and symbolism of Matsya Avatar, the first Dashavatara form of Vishnu.

aeo.shortAnswer: Matsya is Vishnu's fish avatar, remembered for protecting life and sacred wisdom during a flood.

title: Kurma Avatar
summary: Vishnu becomes Kurma, the tortoise, supporting Mount Mandara during the churning of the ocean.
kidSummary: Kurma teaches that great work needs a steady foundation.

seo.title: Kurma Avatar and Samudra Manthana: Story and Meaning
seo.description: Understand Kurma Avatar, Vishnu's tortoise form, and the meaning of the churning of the ocean.

aeo.shortAnswer: Kurma is Vishnu's tortoise avatar who supports the mountain during Samudra Manthana.

title: Narasimha Avatar
summary: Vishnu appears as Narasimha, neither man nor animal, to protect Prahlada and end Hiranyakashipu's tyranny.
kidSummary: Narasimha shows that devotion is protected even when fear seems powerful.

seo.title: Narasimha Avatar Story: Prahlada, Protection, and Meaning
seo.description: Read the Narasimha story with meaning, source notes, quiz, and drawing activities.

aeo.shortAnswer: Narasimha is Vishnu's man-lion avatar who protects Prahlada and defeats Hiranyakashipu.
